- 15 games are still ahead on the schedule, keeping the focus on pregame matchup context.
- 15 upcoming games currently list probable starters on both sides.

- A healthy number of listed probable starters gives reporters stronger footing for preview coverage before first pitch.
- With finals already posting, the board supports both quick-turn recap writing and forward-looking setup for the remaining slate.
- An overnight MLB edition helps preserve late West Coast results while still setting up the next day's board.

- Where probable starters are confirmed, pitching becomes the clearest pregame frame for reporters looking to separate signal from schedule noise.
- A split-day board creates room for rolling coverage, with early recaps feeding directly into later preview and live-update cycles.

True shooting percentage offers a cleaner read on shot-quality conversion than raw field goal percentage alone.
Turnover rate and defensive rating help identify which teams are more likely to survive tighter postseason-style possessions.
story_angles: Playoff-context framing: pace, shot efficiency, and turnover control become more useful editorial signals as the postseason environment tightens.
Matchup framing: use net rating gaps to separate true team-strength edges from simple win-loss record noise.

key_data_points: The NFL advanced layer is anchored to 2025 regular-season team efficiency data and 2026 draft-pick structure.
New England Patriots led the available EPA/play board at 0.149.
New England Patriots set the strongest pass-efficiency signal in the available data at 0.324 pass EPA per play.

Los Angeles Rams checked in at 0.138 EPA per play.
Buffalo Bills checked in at 0.133 EPA per play.

Los Angeles Rams generated 0.252 pass EPA per play.

why_it_matters: EPA/play gives a stronger snapshot of down-to-down offensive value than raw yardage alone.
Passing EPA and rushing EPA help separate where teams are creating value instead of relying on surface-level totals.
Draft-pick concentration matters because multiple early selections can shift roster construction, trade flexibility, and positional strategy.
In non-game windows, draft structure and the previous season’s efficiency profile are often the clearest editorial anchors for NFL analysis.
story_angles: Rushing-structure angle: the Baltimore Ravens bring one of the strongest available run-game efficiency profiles at 0.083 rush EPA per play.
Quarterback and offensive-line stories usually gain the most traction near the draft, but EPA-based efficiency helps separate true team-building need from surface-level narrative.
During the season, this file can shift from draft framing into matchup framing by using the prior efficiency profile as a baseline.
